Image Resize

Resize any JPG, PNG or WEBP image to exact dimensions. Set a preset or enter custom width and height. Your file is processed entirely in your browser and never uploaded.

No Signup
Browser-based
Free to use
Privacy-first
Files stay on your device. Not uploaded anywhere.

Drop your image here or click to choose

Supports JPG, PNG and WEBP

Max 20MB per file

Advertisement

Ad space reserved for future

Advertisement space

Ad space reserved for future

What is Image Resize?

Image Resize lets you change the width and height of a photo or image to any dimension you need. Whether you need a specific pixel size for an online form or want to reduce dimensions to save space, this tool does it instantly in your browser.

How to Resize an Image

  1. Click "Choose File" or drag your image into the upload area
  2. Select a preset size or enter your own width and height in pixels
  3. Toggle aspect ratio lock to maintain proportions automatically
  4. Choose your output format (JPG, PNG or WEBP) and quality
  5. Click "Resize Image" and download the result

Common Resize Sizes for Indian Forms

  • Passport / Visa photo: 35mm × 45mm (approximately 413 × 531 px at 300 DPI)
  • SSC / UPSC / State PSC exams: Usually 200×230 px or 3.5cm × 4.5cm, under 50KB
  • NEET, JEE, CUET: Check the notification — typically 3.5×4.5cm, 10KB–200KB
  • Job portals (Naukri, LinkedIn): 400×400 px square is safe
  • Aadhaar enrolment: Typically 200×200 px, under 200KB

Tips for Best Results

  • Use JPG format for photos — it gives the best file-size-to-quality ratio
  • Use PNG for images with sharp edges, text, or transparent backgrounds
  • For government forms, always check the official notification for the exact size and file size limit
  • If the portal gives a dimension in cm, use 300 DPI to calculate pixels (e.g. 3.5cm = ~413px)
  • Do not enlarge a small image — this will make it blurry without adding detail

Image Resize — Frequently Asked Questions
